title: When the CWD does not exist, podman compose will report that podman is not installed.
body:
### Issue Description

Podman version:       5.4.2

Running `podman compose` with a current working directory that doesn't exist anymore gives the improper error ```CRITICAL:podman_compose:it seems that you do not have `podman` installed```





### Steps to reproduce the issue

(fish shell, output commented)

```fish
echo "
services:
  redis:
     image: 'redis:alpine'
 " > compose.yaml
podman compose up -d
# >>>> Executing external compose provider "/usr/bin/podman-compose". Please see podman-compose(1) for how to disable this message. <<<<

# 884cdee99ddbe3ab6b86cbe2834a23f0ff22660091ff89a96786f893a87e8f41
# c6d6a9877d442b7a4ec0e3d7f96aa89019af13d90bb8d5eb029a58ec79ac3372
# pod-ct_redis_1
podman compose down
#>>>> Executing external compose provider "/usr/bin/podman-compose". Please see podman-compose(1) for how to disable this message. <<<<

#pod-ct_redis_1
#pod-ct_redis_1
# 884cdee99ddbe3ab6b86cbe2834a23f0ff22660091ff89a96786f893a87e8f41
# pod-ct_default
rm -rf ~/pod-ct
# NOTE: ~/pod-ct was the CWD
podman compose up
#>>>> Executing external compose provider "/usr/bin/podman-compose". Please see podman-compose(1) for how to disable this message. <<<<

#CRITICAL:podman_compose:it seems that you do not have `podman` installed
#Error: executing /usr/bin/podman-compose up: exit status 1

podman info
# error getting current working directory: No such file or directory
```

### Describe the results you received

improper error: ```CRITICAL:podman_compose:it seems that you do not have `podman` installed```

### Describe the results you expected

A proper error would report that the CWD does not exist, or some functionality would not rely on the CWD existing.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

fedora 40 in virtualbox

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Hi @mdemoss 

Thank you for the detailed bug report! This issue is actually with how podman-compose handles checking if podman is installed. Can you please re-open this in the [podman-compose project](https://github.com/containers/podman-compose)

You can include this link to the issue:
https://github.com/containers/podman-compose/blob/77a7963ebeb067706a145cbda7f7c124cfb4afc1/podman_compose.py#L2141

Currently the python exception handling is just too broad, handling for this situation can be added in this block.
